language: Route A Arb scaffold: float D/N + relative mid/rad + GLB sketch + candidate N(λ) shape + truncation formulae. Addresses counting gap; not certified.

at_1.note: Candidate integer interval from float D/N on K_Y only. Not Arb/Rump; truncation to Γ\H³ not enclosed. Do not claim N(λ)=[0,0] for dual cert.

at_first_D.note: Candidate integer interval from float D/N on K_Y only. Not Arb/Rump; truncation to Γ\H³ not enclosed. Do not claim N(λ)=[0,0] for dual cert.

truncation.language: Scaffold only — not a proved truncation theorem. Needed to close Route A bracketing_proved + artificial_boundary.
proof_obligations: Enclose A_cut / C_Sob / A_met in Arb on the true core geometry
Or: compare certified D/N spectra on K_Y and K_{Y'} and take limit
Feed Δ_trunc into N(λ) integer interval enclosure

compact_core: Mesh built at engineering height Y=1.25 (product collar above Y is classical for Picard Humbert; Y itself is not Arb-certified as optimal).
product_mesh: Picard mesh is product-like: (x1,x2)-grid extruded in height with floor lift (Lemma G). Top layer is flat y=Y — product collar ready. Non-product floor error handled by lift, not by Arb enclosure of collar mismatch.
dn_guaranteed: Float D/N + relative mid/rad on Q,M. Not Arb assembly; not Rump.
bracketing_proved: Discrete float [λ_k^N, λ_k^D] reported as candidate intervals only. No proof of bracketing with truncation-error constants.
artificial_boundary: Scaffold mid/rad + trunc constants present (route_A_arb_scaffold / truncation_constants_scaffold). Still no proved Δ_trunc enclosure.
counting_enclosure: Candidate N(1.0)=[0, 1] from float brackets (YELLOW). Not certified integer interval.
